commit.message:
Merge pull request #128 from dchu3/fix/x402-fee-payer

fix: use CAIP-2 network identifiers for x402 payment requirements

The x402 facilitator uses CAIP-2 chain IDs (e.g. solana:5eykt4UsFv8P8NJdTREpY1vzqKqZKvdp
for mainnet). Map SERVER_SOLANA_NETWORK to the correct CAIP-2 ID so that
the payment requirements contain the canonical network identifier that
the x402 SDK and facilitator understand.

Accepts both legacy names (solana, solana-devnet) and CAIP-2 IDs directly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: commit historical game CSVs; scrape only current season in CI

- Carve out gitignore exceptions for games_2021-2025.csv (1.2-1.5MB each)
- CI now always uses fast path: scrape --seasons 2026 only (~30s)
- Full rescrape available via workflow_dispatch full_rescrape=true
- Remove processed-data Actions cache (no longer needed)
- Historical CSVs never change; committing them eliminates the O(27500)
  per-game HTTP request problem that caused 20+ min scrape times

commit.message:
Update app/Http/Controllers/Dashboard/PermissionController.php

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add roadmap and implementation plans for Cortex feature development (#40)

* docs(analysis): add Cortex vs Claude-Mem functional comparison and roadmap

- docs/analysis/claude-mem-comparison.md: full feature matrix comparing
  Cortex and Claude-Mem across memory model, storage/search, CLI/MCP
  tools, advanced features, integrations, and configuration.
  Includes gap summary and strategic analysis.
- docs/analysis/roadmap.md: tiered roadmap (Tier 1A optimisation,
  Tier 1B UX/privacy, Tier 2 core parity, Tier 3 infrastructure)
  with motivation, scope, implementation notes, acceptance criteria,
  effort estimates, and a "How to Extend" guide for future contributors.
- docs/INDEX.md: added Analysis & Roadmap section.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01N6WYzHEuQcWhsMLhkgnrzk

* Plan: 8 individual functional implementation plans for Cortex roadmap tiers

Plans cover 8 features across 4 tiers:
- Tier 1A: Token-aware search, Timeline tool, Summarisation
- Tier 1B: Privacy tags, TUI Memory Browser
- Tier 2: SQLite+FTS5 backend, IDE lifecycle hooks
- Tier 3: Storage performance benchmarks, Multi-project memory

Key design decisions:
- Summarisation tool uses thinking-prompt pattern (consistent with cortex_review_session)
- TUI browser uses Charm packages (bubbletea) with Space/o/e/d multi-select operations
- IDE hooks improve existing session-start/session-stop + add UserPromptSubmit
- SQLite backend uses modernc.org/sqlite (pure Go, no CGo)

Implementation order enables parallel work in Tier 1A/1B before Tier 2/3.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01N6WYzHEuQcWhsMLhkgnrzk

* fix(docs): align roadmap.md with implementation plans — thinking-prompt, TUI, IDE hooks (#41)

* Initial plan

* fix(docs): align roadmap.md with implementation plans - thinking-prompt, TUI, hooks

Co-authored-by: fmatsos <805227+fmatsos@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: increase default heap to 64MB, add --heap-size and --gc-entries flags

- Default heap increased from 1MB to 64MB for x86/x86_64 (fixes crashes
  in long-running server applications due to heap overflow)
- GC table entries increased from 8,192 to 65,536 across all architectures
- New --heap-size=<size> flag accepts values like 64m, 128M, 1g, or raw bytes
- New --gc-entries=<N> flag for configurable GC table size
- ARM64 mmap-backed heap unaffected (already dynamic/growable)
- Added install.sh for automated global installation
- Built binaries for darwin/arm64, darwin/amd64, linux/amd64, linux/arm64,
  windows/amd64
- Version bumped to V0.1.4
- All existing tests updated and passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: throttle popover scroll handlers and scope ChatPopup resize listeners (#3177)

* Initial plan

* fix: throttle popover scroll handlers with RAF and scope ChatPopup resize listeners

Root cause: Layout thrashing from unthrottled getBoundingClientRect() + setState()
calls in scroll event handlers caused the main thread to stall during rapid scroll,
producing intermittent scroll freezing.

- Wrap scroll/resize position recalculations in requestAnimationFrame across 5
  components (NotificationBell, AddAgentPopover, ChoreCard, ModelSelector, StageCard)
  to coalesce layout reads to once per frame
- Move ChatPopup's window-level mousemove/mouseup listeners from always-on to
  only active during resize operations, eliminating unnecessary event dispatching

Co-authored-by: Boykai <9115570+Boykai@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add tests for scroll handler throttling and resize listener scoping

Co-authored-by: Boykai <9115570+Boykai@users.noreply.github.com>

* Merge judge changes into copilot/fix-scroll-freezing-issue

* Initial plan

* review: adopt justified PR feedback

- Wrap flushRAF() callback execution in act() to suppress React state-update warnings and prevent flaky assertions

Evaluated against PR review recommendations.

Co-authored-by: Boykai <9115570+Boykai@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Boykai <9115570+Boykai@users.noreply.github.com>

* Merge linter changes into copilot/fix-scroll-freezing-issue

* Initial plan

* style: fix Prettier formatting in scroll throttling and resize scoping changes

Co-authored-by: Boykai <9115570+Boykai@users.noreply.github.com>

both GitHub Actions and Azure DevOps pipelines to deploy side-by-side
into isolated resource groups (rg-foundry-github-{env} vs
rg-foundry-ado-{env}) within the same subscription.

fix: use CAIP-2 network identifiers for x402 payment requirements

The x402 facilitator uses CAIP-2 chain IDs (e.g. solana:5eykt4UsFv8P8NJdTREpY1vzqKqZKvdp
for mainnet). Map SERVER_SOLANA_NETWORK to the correct CAIP-2 ID so that
the payment requirements contain the canonical network identifier that
the x402 SDK and facilitator understand.

Accepts both legacy names (solana, solana-devnet) and CAIP-2 IDs directly.

commit.message:
feat(react): Add ChatProvider auth options and external auth timeout

Add authOptions prop to ChatProvider that threads UseAuthStatusOptions
through React context, letting built-in auth UI and custom components
consume openAuthUrl via useAuthOptions() without per-hook wiring.

Add externalAuthTimeoutMs option (default 5 min) that auto-cancels
pending logins when auth launches in an external browser with no
popup handle to detect abandonment. cancelLogin() remains available
as a manual override.

Closes #50
Closes #51

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Xcode management — GUI + CLI (#99)

* feat: Add Xcode management to Apple Tools page

- Add IXcodeService interface with discovery, selection, and download support
- Add XcodeInstallation, XcodeRelease, and related model records
- Implement XcodeService: discovers /Applications/Xcode*.app, parses
  versions via xcodebuild, fetches xcodereleases.com API, switches
  active Xcode via osascript/xcode-select, opens Apple download page
- Add GetInstalledXcodesRequest/Handler with 30s cache
- Add GetAvailableXcodesRequest/Handler with 10min cache + offline
- Rewrite AppleToolsViewModel with real data loading, selection, and
  download support via mediator
- Rewrite AppleTools.razor with Installed/Available tabs, version cards
  showing build number, date, SDKs, compilers, select/download actions,
  proper light+dark mode theming using CSS variables
- Register IXcodeService, handlers in MauiProgram.cs DI

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Xcode switching and download navigation

- Fix xcode-select switching by using ProcessStartInfo.ArgumentList
  instead of Arguments string to prevent nested quote mangling in
  osascript commands
- Fix download to open direct .xip URL from xcodereleases.com
  (Safari handles Apple Developer auth) instead of search page
- Add Xcode nav link to MainLayout sidebar under APPLE section

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add real Xcode download with Apple ID authentication

Implement in-app Apple Developer sign-in (Apple ID + password + 2FA)
for downloading Xcode .xip files directly, replacing the browser-open
approach.

- Add IAppleDownloadAuthService interface and implementation
  - Apple ID sign-in via idmsa.apple.com
  - Trusted device 2FA and SMS verification
  - Olympus session creation for ADCDownloadAuth cookies
  - HTTP download with progress reporting
- Update XcodeService.DownloadXcodeAsync for real authenticated downloads
- Add sign-in modal to AppleTools.razor with 2FA code input
- Wire download through OperationModal with progress tracking
- Show auth status in status bar with sign-out option

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: Remove Apple auth/xcodes download complexity

Download button now opens Apple Developer URL in the browser,
letting the user download and install Xcode themselves.

Removed: IAppleDownloadAuthService, AppleDownloadAuthService,
AppleSignInResult, XcodeDownloadProgress, sign-in modal, 2FA flow,
and xcodes CLI dependency. ~940 lines removed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add Xcode management CLI commands

New subcommands under 'maui-sherpa apple xcode':
- list: Show all installed Xcode versions with active indicator
- available: Browse releases from xcodereleases.com (--beta, --limit)
- select: Switch active Xcode by version or path (admin privileges)
- download: Open Apple Developer download page for a version

All commands support --json for machine-readable output.
Default (no subcommand) still shows active Xcode info.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
RNN quality leap: pre-norm + input injection → val loss 1.94

Key architectural changes to RecurrentCharLM:
- Pre-layer RMSNorm: normalize h before each matmul to prevent
  hidden state explosion through 32 residual layers
- Input injection at every layer: h = h + ReLU(RMSNorm(h) @ W + embed + b)
  gives each layer direct access to the current character embedding
- Post-layer RMSNorm: stabilize hidden state between character steps
- Reduced spectral norm clamping frequency (every 20 batches)

Results (32-layer, 545K params, 3 epochs on Shakespeare):
  Val loss: 3.08 → 1.94 (perplexity 21.8 → 6.9)
  Nearly matches transformer baseline (1.89, 818K params)
  Generated text is now recognizably Shakespearean English

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

sync title and aria-label for toggle play mode button

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: MCP server for Azure Entra PIM

Implements an MCP server with two tools:
- list_eligible: Lists all eligible PIM group and Entra role assignments
  with structured JSON output (outputSchema)
- activate: Activates PIM assignments by name or ID with justification

Features:
- Interactive browser auth with persistent token caching via AuthenticationRecord
- XDG-compliant config storage via env-paths
- undici interceptor to fix Node.js v24 Accept-Language: * header
- Microsoft Graph SDK (Kiota) for all API calls
- Structured MCP responses with outputSchema validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: release workflow with Helm chart and GHCR publishing (#15)

* feat: release workflow with Helm chart and GHCR publishing

Add a release workflow triggered by semantic version tags (v*.*.*):

Release artifacts:
- containerd-shim-cloudhv-v1 (linux/amd64)
- cloudhv-agent (linux/amd64, static musl)
- vmlinux (guest kernel)
- rootfs.ext4 (guest rootfs with crun)
- SHA-256 checksums

OCI artifacts pushed to GHCR:
- ghcr.io/devigned/cloudhv-installer:<tag> (installer image)
- ghcr.io/devigned/charts/cloudhv-installer:<version> (Helm chart)

Helm chart (charts/cloudhv-installer/):
- DaemonSet: installs shim, kernel, rootfs onto nodes
- RuntimeClass: registers cloudhv handler with scheduling and overhead
- Configurable via values.yaml (image, nodeSelector, resources)

Release notes auto-generated from conventional commits between tags,
grouped by type (features, fixes, performance, docs, etc.).

Usage:
  git tag v0.1.0 && git push origin v0.1.0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add releasing guide with Helm chart and GHCR details

Documents the full release process:
- Creating a release (git tag + push)
- What gets published (binaries, OCI images, Helm chart)
- Auto-generated release notes from conventional commits
- Installing from a release (bare Linux, Helm, container image)
- Helm chart values reference
- Workflow job dependency diagram

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Address PR review feedback

- Fix stale closure in handleDone: use functional setScores(prev => ...)
  to avoid dropped updates when completing tasks quickly
- Fix getMonday timezone: use local date formatting instead of
  toISOString() which shifts across UTC boundary
- Fix TaskCard edit sync: useEffect resyncs editTitle/editDescription
  when task prop changes (avoids stale UI after external updates)
- Fix accessibility: editable title/description use role=button,
  tabIndex=0, and Enter/Space keyboard handlers
- Fix review totalPoints: compute from task.points sum at creation
  instead of relying on score tracker (handles deleted tasks)
- Fix backward compat: Task.points and ReviewSnapshot.totalPoints
  are now optional (existing JSON without these fields won't break)
- Fix IPC channels: add scores:get and scores:delete to IpcChannels

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix nginx crash on HAOS: generate self-contained config, avoid all user-switching syscalls (#23)

* Initial plan

* Fix nginx crash on HAOS: self-contained config, run as root, single-process mode

The HA addon container has restricted capabilities (AppArmor nginx_service
sub-profile lacks capability chown/setgid/setuid). Previous attempts failed:
- v1.24.2.1: Permission denied on /var/lib/nginx paths
- v1.24.2.2: chown to UID 100 (nginx) blocked
- v1.24.2.3: s6-setuidgid/initgroups blocked

Fix: Generate a self-contained nginx.conf that bypasses all Alpine defaults.
Use `user root;` to avoid chown/setuid to the nginx user, `access_log off;`
to avoid the compiled-in access log path, `-e stderr` to redirect early
startup logs, and `master_process off;` to run in single-process mode
(no worker forking, no initgroups/setgid calls).

Tested with --cap-drop=ALL --cap-add=NET_BIND_SERVICE to verify it works
even with severely restricted capabilities.

Co-authored-by: Crafter-Y <71288761+Crafter-Y@users.noreply.github.com>

* Set restrictive permissions (700) on /tmp/nginx runtime directory

Co-authored-by: Crafter-Y <71288761+Crafter-Y@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: include facilitator feePayer in x402 payment requirements

The x402 SVM SDK requires extra.feePayer in payment requirements — the
Solana address that pays transaction fees when the facilitator settles.
The server was sending extra: null, causing client-side payment creation
to fail with 'feePayer is required in paymentRequirements.extra'.

Changes:
- Fetch facilitator feePayer from /supported endpoint at startup
- Include it in the payment requirements extra field
- Make buildPaymentRequirements() async; wrap server startup in main()
- Falls back to wildcard signers if no exact network match found

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix 504 timeout: async job processing with polling

- Upload POST now returns a job_id immediately, analysis runs in a
  background thread
- Client polls GET /status/<job_id> every 2s until result is ready
- Eliminates Codespaces proxy 504 gateway timeout (analysis takes
  ~30s but no single HTTP request blocks that long)
- Status text updates during polling to show progress

Initial commit: Mycelium research corpus

11 research reports and 4 whitepapers exploring federated AI agent
orchestration on decentralized social protocols (AT Protocol,
ActivityPub, Matrix).

75,000+ words covering: agent identity (DIDs), data sovereignty (PDS),
capability schemas (Lexicons), federation (Firehose), encrypted
coordination (Matrix/Olm/Megolm), intelligence infrastructure
(decentralized inference), community governance (labelers/boundaries),
and the full spectrum of agent social life beyond work.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: increase weather damage to make game meaningful

- BASE_DAMAGE=5 ensures every tick causes damage regardless of weather
- Rain multiplier 10x (was 2x), wind divisor 3 (was 5)
- Block HP reduced: dry_sand=25, wet_sand=40, packed_sand=60 (was 40/60/100)
- MAX_HEALTH=60, REINFORCE_AMOUNT=15
- Weather fetch now uses max(rain, precipitation) to catch drizzle
- dry_sand survives ~5 calm ticks, packed_sand ~12 — meaningful decay

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
